resources.string: Buy and sell old bicycles with a clean mobile flow.
This first build focuses on the essential journey: sign in, browse bikes, open product detail, preview wishlist, and follow orders.
Demo credentials are prefilled so you can jump straight into the interface.
Login failed. Check your backend status and credentials.
Could not reach the backend. You can still open the demo mode.
Browse live listings with a clear fallback when backend sync is unavailable
Sync your saved bikes when a backend session is active
Track backend order states with clear loading and error feedback
Account snapshot and next setup steps
Ride-ready listings, shaped for mobile first.
This screen now syncs against `/api/products` while still keeping a readable demo fallback when your local backend is offline.
A short vertical feed with clean cards and fast tap targets.
Your shortlist stays compact and easy to compare.
Sign in with a backend account to sync real saved products. Demo mode still keeps the screen previewable.
Each card still opens the same product detail experience.
From deposit request to received confirmation.
This section reads `/api/orders/me` when a backend session is active and falls back to preview cards in demo mode.
Focused status cards keep the timeline readable on a phone screen.
The profile screen is intentionally simple: identity, trust context, and clear exit action.
Sign in with a backend account to sync wishlist changes.
Sign in with a backend buyer account to create an order.
This preview item still uses local demo data. The layout stays ready for the same wishlist and order actions you will use with live backend products.
This listing is coming from the backend. Saving to wishlist and opening orders now follows the same mobile flow used by the authenticated tabs.
The app is checking the backend product feed. Demo cards stay visible while this request is in flight.
The live product endpoint responded without listings, so this section is staying honest instead of repeating demo content.
The product API could not be reached. The screen keeps the last available cards visible so browsing does not suddenly collapse.
Fetching `/api/wishlist` for the current signed-in account.
Your backend wishlist is empty right now. Save a product from its detail screen to make this tab useful.
Wishlist sync failed. Retry after confirming your backend is running and your token is still valid.
You opened the app without a backend session, so these saved cards are local preview data only.
Fetching `/api/orders/me` for the active backend account.
The current account does not have any orders to display. Once an order exists, this tab will show its latest status here.
Order sync failed. Retry after checking backend auth and API availability.
You are still in demo mode, so these cards are local examples and not your real backend order history.
Live order flow requires a backend product.
Sign in with a backend account before creating an order.
Request %1$s upfront for a bike priced at %2$s.
Request the full listing price of %1$s.
The default suggestion is around 20%% of the listing price, which is currently %1$s. You can adjust it, but it must stay above 0 and not exceed the full price.
Full payment means the backend will treat %1$s as the required upfront amount.
Enter an upfront amount for partial payment.
Upfront amount must be greater than 0.
Upfront amount cannot exceed the listing price.
Use digits only for the upfront amount.
The mobile app is asking the backend to create a new order for this product.
Order request created. Opening your orders.
